agent_raw: The evaluation methodology is sound. This is a selective-prediction system where the model either answers tickets above a confidence threshold or routes them to human agents. The correct evaluation for such a system is exactly what was done: report performance on auto-resolved tickets plus coverage of total volume. The pre-registered 0.92 threshold directly addresses the concern that performance metrics appear favorable because hard tickets are excluded — the threshold was not selected to cherry-pick; it was set by legal and operations stakeholders to meet a 1% harmful-error requirement before the model was developed. The untouched test set confirmed both the harmful-error ceiling (0.7%) and the coverage floor (37% exceeding the 30% minimum). The claim is correctly scoped to the abstaining system. A critic might argue that the evaluation does not characterize performance on the 63% of abstained tickets, but for a system designed to route those to humans, that is not a gap in the evaluation — it is the system's intended behavior.
